kwargs.content:
Okay — here’s what I found and what I can do next.

Summary of findings
- I searched the fastest route from your current location (Monaco) to Andorra la Vella and selected the fastest route (via B388, K679).
- There is one suitable charging station along that route: EDF Pulse at about route kilometer 150 (detour ≈ 8.8 km, detour time ≈ 12 min). It’s open 00:00–24:00 and has an available DC 350 kW plug.
- Estimated drive time to that charging station from now (current time 17:10) is 1 hour 56 minutes, so expected arrival is about 19:06 — inside your requested window of 19:00–19:45.
- I looked for fast food near that stop. The only fast-food POI at the same route kilometer is Brioche Dorée, but it closes at 18:00, so it would not be open at ~19:06. In short: the charging station is fine for charging at your requested time, but there is no fast-food place open right at that charging stop during 19:00–19:45.

Options
- A) I set navigation now with EDF Pulse as a charging stop (arrival ~19:06). This meets your time window and guarantees a charging stop; however there is no open fast-food at that stop.
- B) I skip an en-route charging stop and set direct navigation to Andorra la Vella — there are open fast-food options in Andorra la Vella on arrival (MásQMenos open until 22:00, Telepizza until 24:00).
- C) I can keep looking for another charging station further along the route that has a fast-food place open at that time, but my searches found no other charging stations along the fastest route segment windows I checked.

Which do you want me to do? If you want option A, say “yes” and I’ll set navigation with EDF Pulse as the charging waypoint.

Got it — I kept searching along the fastest route for a charging station that actually has an open fast-food place at the same stop between 19:00 and 19:45.

What I checked
- I searched route kilometers between about 150 and 210 (that range matches arrival times between ~19:00 and ~19:45 from your current time).
- Results:
  - EDF Pulse (route km 150): charging station is open 24/7 and has an available DC 350 kW plug. There is a fast-food POI listed at the same route kilometer (Brioche Dorée), but Brioche Dorée closes at 18:00 — so it would NOT be open at ~19:00.
  - Engie Electric (route km 200): charging station is open 24/7 (DC 300 kW plug, currently occupied in the data). There is a McDonald’s listed near route km 200 that is open until 23:00. However the data shows different detour distances: the charging station detour is about 4.2 km, McDonald’s detour is about 7.4 km. That means McDonald’s is nearby but not located at the same charging-station POI (so the stop would be the charging station and the fast-food would require an additional detour).

Conclusion
- I could NOT find any charging station along the route with a fast-food place that is both on-site and open between 19:00 and 19:45.
- The closest match is Engie Electric at route km 200 with a McDonald’s nearby that is open at that time — but it is not listed as being at the same POI (so it’s not “at the charging station” according to the POI data).

How would you like to proceed?
- Option 1: Take Engie Electric (route km 200) as your charging stop — it meets your time window and there’s a McDonald’s nearby open at that time. I can set navigation with Engie Electric as the charging waypoint.
- Option 2: Take EDF Pulse (route km 150) — charging spot arrives a little earlier (~19:06) but the fast-food at that exact stop is closed.
- Option 3: Widen the time window (earlier or later) so I can search more widely for an on-site fast-food at a charger.
- Option 4: Skip en-route charging and plan to eat at Andorra la Vella (there are open fast-food places on arrival).

Tell me which option you want, or say “keep looking” if you want me to search other route alternatives (I can check the second/third route options too).

Nice — I checked the other route alternatives you asked for.

What I checked and found
I scanned the second route (the one that includes tolls) and the third route for chargers around the same travel-time window (roughly arrival between 19:00 and 19:45). The results are the same pattern on all alternatives: EDF Pulse shows up on each route at about route km 150 (open 24/7, has an available DC 350 kW plug) but the fast-food at that same stop (Brioche Dorée) closes at 18:00, so it would not be open between 19:00 and 19:45. I also found Engie Electric around route km 200 (open 24/7) and there is a McDonald’s listed nearby that is open until 23:00 — but McDonald’s is not the same POI as the charger, it’s a separate detour (charging station detour ≈ 4.2 km, McDonald’s detour ≈ 7.4 km).

Bottom line
Across the fastest, second and third route alternatives I could not find any charging station that has an on-site fast-food place that is open between 19:00 and 19:45. The best practical matches are chargers with a nearby fast-food place (Engie Electric + McDonald’s), but that requires a short extra detour from the charger.

How do you want to proceed?
- I can set navigation to Engie Electric as the charging stop and include the nearby McDonald’s as a short extra detour.
- I can set navigation to EDF Pulse (earlier arrival ~19:06) even though the on-site fast food is closed.
- I can keep searching other kilometers or check beyond the current time window (but that will likely push arrival later than 19:45).
- Or you can widen the time window and I’ll search again for a true on-site open fast-food at a charger.

Say which option you prefer or tell me to keep looking (and whether to widen the time window).

Done — navigation is set with Engie Electric as a charging stop and Andorra la Vella as final destination. Your route and stops
- Start: Monaco (current location)
- Waypoint 1: Engie Electric (charging stop, route detour ≈ 4.2 km)
- Final destination: Andorra la Vella

Nice — navigation is active now and includes the charging stop. There’s a McDonald’s nearby Engie Electric that's open until 23:00; it's a short extra detour from the charger. Want me to add the McDonald's as a driving waypoint too so the system will route you there automatically after charging?

I tried to add McDonald’s as a waypoint after Engie Electric, but hit a system limitation.

What I did and what failed
- I checked the current navigation (Monaco → Engie Electric → Andorra la Vella) and looked up routes needed to insert poi_fas_776135 (McDonald’s) between Engie and Andorra.
- The navigation API requires a valid route that starts at the waypoint before the new waypoint (i.e., a route that starts at Engie and ends at McDonald’s). The routing service does not support requesting routes between two POIs, so I could not obtain an Engie→McDonald’s route to supply to the navigation_add_one_waypoint tool.
- Because I couldn’t get a route from Engie to McDonald’s, the system will not accept adding McDonald’s as an intermediate waypoint while keeping Engie as the prior waypoint.

Options you can pick from
- Option A (recommended): Keep the current navigation (Monaco → Engie Electric → Andorra la Vella). When you finish charging at Engie, tell me “Add McDonald’s now” and I’ll set McDonald’s as the next waypoint at that moment (I can do the small manual step while you’re stopped). This preserves the charging stop and then routes you to McDonald’s after charging.
- Option B: Replace the current navigation now with Monaco → McDonald’s → Andorra la Vella (I can set this immediately). Note: this would remove Engie Electric from the active route (so it would not be a planned charging stop).
- Option C: I can cancel navigation and try a different approach (but the same POI→POI route limitation will likely block inserting McDonald’s after Engie in one operation).

Which do you want? If you choose Option A, say “I’ll add McDonald’s after charging” and I’ll wait for your go-ahead at the charger and then add it. If you want me to switch to Option B or try something else, say so and I’ll proceed.

Perfect — I’ll wait and add McDonald’s after you finish charging.

Quick recap: navigation is already set Monaco → Engie Electric → Andorra la Vella. Drive to Engie Electric and you should arrive around 19:40 (that’s within your 19:00–19:45 window). Engie Electric is open 24/7 but the listed DC plug was occupied in the info I saw (availability can change). McDonald’s nearby is open until 23:00 and requires a short extra detour (McDonald’s detour ≈ 7.4 km from the route; Engie detour ≈ 4.2 km).

When you’re at the charger, just say “Add McDonald’s now” and I’ll add it as the next waypoint and route you there. Sound good?

- Set passenger seat heating to level 3.
- Set fan speed to 2.

For Frankfurt to Cologne (fastest): it goes via L399, L319, distance 179.1 km, estimated duration 2 hours 13 minutes, no tolls. That leg is well within the 342 km usable range, so it’s fine once you have adequate charge at Frankfurt.

How I estimated your state when you get there: right now you’re at 65% SOC and the car reports 494 km remaining range. That implies about 7.6 km per percent. After ~250 km you’d be at roughly 32% SOC on arrival at that charger.

Charging times I checked at that station:
- Using the 150 kW DC plug: 32% → 49% in about 5 minutes; 32% → 80% in about 15 minutes.
- Using the 50 kW DC plug: 32% → 49% in about 15 minutes.

Practical note for the trip: the total Hamburg→Frankfurt leg is 466 km. From the charger (at km 250) you’d have about 216 km left to Frankfurt.
